description: Someone makes a bowl from clay, soup from ingredients, or peace through what people do together. 'pilewa' brings about a result through work with the material or conditions at hand. 'phoa' (do) names an action without requiring a produced result, while 'kealo' (create) centres something new coming into existence.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and let air pass along its sides into stressed middle-height front 'e', then round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for 'w' before opening into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: One stop opens into a lateral and then a glide; after the first release, the word keeps moving. Its vowels widen towards final 'a', giving the finish an open-handed ease.
usage_notes: The result made is the direct object. Material, instrument, recipient, and purpose take their ordinary phrases. The event noun can name the making or its result when context makes the reading clear.

semantic_domains.activity: work that brings about a concrete or abstract result
semantic_domains.creation: making an object, preparation, or shared condition
